title: Stedelijk Museum
full_description: The Stedelijk Museum is the Netherlands' largest museum of modern and contemporary art and design, with a collection of 100,000 objects from 1870 to the present. Major holdings represent Bauhaus, De Stijl, CoBrA, neo-impressionism, abstract expressionism, pop art, minimalism, and conceptual art, with works by van Gogh, Kandinsky, Matisse, Pollock, Appel, Warhol, De Kooning, Marlene Dumas, and many others. The 19th-century Weissman building connects to a 21st-century wing ('the Bathtub') designed by Benthem Crouwel Architects.
ai_summary: Amsterdam's great modern art museum on Museumplein — CoBrA, De Stijl, Bauhaus, and Mondrian alongside Pollock, Warhol, and Marlene Dumas, in a 19th-century building that opens into a gleaming contemporary wing spanning over a century of twentieth- and twenty-first-century art.
